enonce.texte: Match the terms on the left with their correct definitions on the right. This exercise tests your knowledge of key healthcare vocabulary from Lesson 31.

questions.texte: Match each term to its definition.

sousQuestions.reponse: c. A large institution where sick or injured people receive medical treatment
e. A substance injected to stimulate the body's immune system against a disease
a. A qualified medical professional who diagnoses and treats illnesses
b. Medical care given to a patient for an illness or injury
d. A healthcare facility, smaller than a hospital, often for outpatients

enonce.texte:
Complete each sentence with the correct word from the box. Use each word only once.

Word box: nurse, medicine, cure, hospital, treatment, doctor, clinic, vaccine

questions.texte: My grandmother has a fever, so I will take her to the local ______.
The ______ prescribed antibiotics for my throat infection.
After the accident, he was rushed to the ______ for emergency surgery.
The ______ administered the injection carefully and checked my blood pressure.
Scientists are working hard to find a ______ for HIV/AIDS.
The COVID-19 ______ has saved millions of lives worldwide.
Always take your ______ exactly as the doctor instructs.
Early ______ for malaria can prevent serious complications.

enonce.texte: Read the dialogue between a patient and a nurse. Complete the missing parts using appropriate healthcare vocabulary from Lesson 31.

sousQuestions.texte: Nurse: Good morning. Welcome to the city ______. How can I help you?
Patient: I have a high fever and a persistent cough. I need to see a ______.
Nurse: Please take a seat. The ______ will call your name. Have you taken any ______ so far?
Patient: Yes, I took some paracetamol, but the fever did not go down.
Nurse: The doctor will prescribe an appropriate ______ after examining you. Have you received the flu ______ this year?

intitule: Real-Life Problem Solving: Choosing Healthcare Services

enonce.texte: Read each scenario carefully. Use your knowledge of healthcare facilities and professionals to recommend the most appropriate action. Justify your answer using vocabulary from Lesson 31.

questions.texte: Scenario 1: Your younger brother has a minor cut on his finger while playing. It is not bleeding heavily. What should you do?
questions.reponse: Clean the wound at home with soap and water, then apply a bandage. There is no need to go to a hospital or clinic for a minor cut unless it shows signs of infection.

questions.texte: Scenario 2: Your mother has had a very high fever (40°C) for three days, and she is confused and weak. What type of healthcare facility should you take her to? Justify.
questions.reponse: You should take her to a hospital immediately. A hospital has emergency services, laboratory tests (to diagnose malaria, typhoid, etc.), and doctors who can provide urgent treatment. A small clinic may not have the equipment or staff for such serious symptoms.

questions.texte: Scenario 3: Your friend has a persistent cough and has been coughing up blood for two weeks. He refuses to see a doctor because he is afraid of the cost. What advice would you give him?
questions.reponse: I would advise him that coughing blood is a serious symptom (possible tuberculosis or other lung disease). He must see a doctor immediately. Many government hospitals and clinics offer free or low-cost treatment for diseases like TB. Delaying treatment can make the disease worse and spread it to others. Early treatment saves lives.

questions.texte: Scenario 4: A government health campaign is offering free vaccines against measles at the local community center. Your neighbor says vaccines are dangerous and refuses to take her child. How would you respond using scientific facts?
questions.reponse: I would respectfully explain that vaccines are safe and scientifically proven to prevent deadly diseases like measles. The measles vaccine has saved millions of children's lives worldwide. Not vaccinating puts her child and other children (including babies too young to be vaccinated) at risk of a serious, preventable disease. I would encourage her to speak with a trusted doctor or nurse for accurate information.

intitule: Sentence Transformation: Active to Passive (Medical Context)

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following sentences from active to passive voice. This exercise combines healthcare vocabulary with passive voice formation (Lesson 15).

questions.texte: The doctor treats the patient every morning.
questions.reponse: The patient is treated by the doctor every morning.

questions.texte: The nurse will administer the vaccine tomorrow.
questions.reponse: The vaccine will be administered by the nurse tomorrow.

questions.texte: Scientists have developed a new cure for malaria.
questions.reponse: A new cure for malaria has been developed by scientists.

questions.texte: The clinic provides free treatment to poor patients.
questions.reponse: Free treatment is provided to poor patients by the clinic. OR Poor patients are provided free treatment by the clinic.

intitule: Writing Task: Letter to a Friend About a Health Issue

enonce.texte:
Write a short letter (150-200 words) to a friend who is feeling unwell but refuses to go to a healthcare facility. In your letter:

  • Express concern using appropriate vocabulary
  • Encourage him/her to see a doctor or nurse
  • Explain why early treatment is important
  • Suggest a specific type of facility (clinic, hospital, etc.)
  • Use at least 8 vocabulary words from Lesson 31

questions.reponse: Sample answer: Dear John, I am very worried about your health. You told me you have had a high fever and a persistent cough for over a week. Please do not ignore these symptoms. You need to see a doctor as soon as possible. A doctor can diagnose what is wrong and prescribe the right medicine. If you delay treatment, your condition could get worse. You can go to the local clinic near your house. The nurses there are very kind, and the consultation is affordable. They may give you a vaccine if needed, or provide other treatment. Early treatment always leads to a faster cure. Do not be afraid. Your health is the most important thing. Please take this advice seriously. Your friend, [Name]
